ClusterServiceRotateCredentialBody.description:
RotateCredentialRequest follows the six-step protocol in design §5.7.3:
Put new credential -> probe with temp client -> CAS switch -> invalidate
pool -> delayed old_ref cleanup. expected_revision guards CAS.

ClusterServiceUpdateClusterBody.description:
UpdateClusterRequest uses FieldMask to avoid PUT clearing unset fields
(design §5.3.2/§5.7.4). expected_revision is the CAS guard; mismatch
returns REVISION_CONFLICT. Immutable fields in update_mask return
INVALID_ARGUMENT.

templateId.title: Hub SandboxTemplate ID; creates from template if set

expectedCommitSha.description: Compare-and-swap guard: publication fails when source_ref moved.

version.description: Accepts SemVer with or without the leading v; the server canonicalizes it.

protobufNullValue.description:
`NullValue` is a singleton enumeration to represent the null value for the
`Value` type union.

The JSON representation for `NullValue` is JSON `null`.

 - NULL_VALUE: Null value.

v1AuditRecord.description: AuditRecord is one security audit event. Mirrors kernel auditx.Record.

fullyConsistent.description:
fully_consistent=true forces SpiceDB to use the latest writes
(slowest). When false, uses at-least-as-fresh with the optional
consistency_token. Default false.

subjectAttrs.description:
Subject / resource / environment attrs are passed to SpiceDB as
caveat context (ABAC). Optional; omit for pure ReBAC checks.

effect.description:
effect is the canonical decision: "allow", "deny", "no_match".
"no_match" means SpiceDB had no matching relationship / permission
definition; callers usually treat it the same as "deny" but may
surface a different message.

v1Cluster.description:
Cluster is the AISphere control-plane resource for a registered Kubernetes
cluster. It is NOT the kubeconfig file. Credential material is never
returned by any RPC (design §5.2).

v1ClusterCredentialInput.description:
ClusterCredentialInput carries the credential supplied by the caller on
create/rotate. Hub never persists plaintext; this message only flows into
CredentialStore.Put. Logs must redact every field (design §5.4).

v1ClusterPermissions.description:
ClusterPermissions is derived from SpiceDB BatchCheck at read time
(design §7.6.2). can_delete is derived from can_manage. Cluster V1 has no
share API, so can_share is intentionally omitted (design §5.2).

v1ClusterStatus.description: ClusterStatus mirrors the lifecycle state machine in design §5.7.1.

v1DeletePolicy.description:
DeletePolicy controls Namespace cleanup when a Cluster is deleted
(design §5.7.5). DETACH_ONLY keeps remote Namespaces; CASCADE removes
AISphere-managed Namespaces too.

v1EffectiveVisibility.description:
EffectiveVisibility is the observed SpiceDB state (design §6.3). PARTIAL
covers partial-wildcard drift; UNKNOWN covers SpiceDB unreachable.

codeVerifier.description:
code_verifier is the PKCE code verifier. Required when the authorization
request was made with a code_challenge; recommended for all public
clients (SPAs, mobile apps) that cannot store a client secret.
Maps to kernel authn.AuthCodeExchangeRequest.CodeVerifier.

NOTE: see Exchange RPC comment — the current Casdoor adapter does not
yet forward this to the SDK. The field is accepted on the wire for
forward compatibility.

v1ExchangeResponse.description:
ExchangeResponse is the OAuth2/OIDC token set returned by Exchange.

`expires_in` is seconds from issue time to expiry (OAuth2 standard). For a
wall-clock expiry, callers can compute issued_at + expires_in, or use the
`expires_at` field on Principal returned by Me/Introspect.

v1InClusterCredential.description:
Uses the Hub pod's service account token. V1 requires AllowInsecureDev
 because Hub is not necessarily in-cluster; reserved for local dev.

audience.description:
audience optionally restricts the accepted token audience. When unset,
the audience check is skipped.

issuer.description:
issuer optionally restricts the accepted token issuer. When unset, the
globally configured IdP issuer is used.

token.description: token is the token to validate and inspect.

tokenType.description:
token_type hints which kind of token is being introspected. Accepted
values: "access_token" (default), "refresh_token", "id_token".
Maps to kernel authn.VerifyTokenRequest.TokenType.

active.description:
active indicates whether the token is currently valid (not expired, not
revoked, signature OK, issuer/audience match if specified).

expiresAt.description: expires_at is the token's expiration time. Zero value means unknown.

issuedAt.description: issued_at is the token's issue time. Zero value means unknown.

scope.description: scope is the space-delimited scope list attached to the token.

v1Namespace.description:
Namespace is the AISphere control-plane record (design §6.3). visibility is
the desired value persisted in DB; effective_visibility reflects SpiceDB
state. Product UI MUST use effective_visibility + visibility_sync_status_enum,
never visibility alone (design §7.5.2).

visibilitySyncStatus.description:
visibility_sync_status is the legacy string form. New code uses
visibility_sync_status_enum (field 23). Kept for backward compatibility.

v1NamespacePermissions.description:
NamespacePermissions is derived from SpiceDB BatchCheck (design §7.6.2).
can_share and can_delete derive from can_manage.

v1Principal.description:
Principal is the normalized identity of the current caller.

Field set is aligned with kernel authn.Principal (github.com/aisphereio/
kernel/authn.Principal). The kernel type has additional internal fields
(Provider, ExternalID, Issuer, Audience, TenantID, AppID, IssuedAt) that
are not exposed here because they are not useful to typical frontend
consumers; if a future use case needs them, add fields rather than
exposing the kernel struct verbatim.

attributes.description:
attributes carries provider-specific claims (e.g. Casdoor owner/name,
external ID, signin method, custom fields) that are not part of the
canonical Principal schema. Values must be JSON-serializable.
Maps to kernel authn.Principal.Attributes.

NOTE: some attributes (such as `access_token`) are sensitive and
SHOULD be filtered out by the service layer before returning to
frontend clients. See kernel/authn/casdoor/token.go:principalFromClaims
for the list of attributes currently set by the Casdoor adapter.

authMethod.description:
auth_method records how the subject authenticated. Common values match
the kernel authn.AuthMethod* constants: "oauth", "oidc", "jwt",
"api_key", "mtls", "session", "dev_token", "none".
Maps to kernel authn.Principal.AuthMethod.

expiresAt.description:
expires_at is when the current access token expires. Frontends should
use this to schedule proactive token refreshes before the token
expires, rather than waiting for a 401 to trigger a reactive refresh.
Zero value means the expiry is unknown (e.g. the IdP did not return
an exp claim).
Maps to kernel authn.Principal.ExpiresAt.

phone.description:
phone is the user's phone number when available from the IdP.
Maps to kernel authn.Principal.Phone.

scopes.description:
scopes are the OAuth/OIDC scopes attached to the current access token.
Frontends can use this to gate UI affordances (e.g. hide "write"
buttons when the token lacks the write scope).
Maps to kernel authn.Principal.Scopes.

total.title: number of records returned (not total in store)

v1RefreshResponse.description: RefreshResponse is the OAuth2/OIDC token set returned by Refresh.

v1Relationship.description:
Relationship is one ReBAC tuple. resource#relation@subject.

For ABAC, optionally set caveat_name + caveat_context. The caveat must
be defined in the SpiceDB schema; caveat_context is a JSON object
whose keys match the caveat's required parameters.

v1RevokeRequest.description: RevokeRequest asks the implementation to revoke a single token.

token.description: token is the access, refresh, or id token to revoke.

tokenType.description:
token_type hints which kind of token is being revoked. Accepted values:
  "access_token"  (default)
  "refresh_token"
  "id_token"
Maps to kernel authn.RevokeTokenRequest.TokenType.

v1RevokeResponse.description:
RevokeResponse returns the outcome of a Revoke call.

We do not use google.protobuf.Empty so callers can distinguish
"IdP-side revoked" from "only local blacklist updated" — important for
security-sensitive flows where the caller needs to know whether the token
may still be valid against other Resource Servers.

idpRevoked.description:
idp_revoked is true when the IdP itself confirmed revocation. False
means only the local session blacklist was updated; the token may still
be valid against other Resource Servers until it expires.

message.description:
message carries a human-readable note, e.g. "casdoor sdk does not expose
token revocation; only local blacklist updated".

revoked.description:
revoked is true when the token was successfully revoked (either IdP-side
or local blacklist).

v1Sandbox.description:
Sandbox is the AISphere control-plane record for an Agent Sandbox instance
(design §11). The remote K8s Sandbox CRD (agents.x-k8s.io/v1beta1) is synced
via SSA; this row is the authoritative Hub-side record.

v1SandboxClaim.description:
SandboxClaim claims a pre-warmed Sandbox from a WarmPool without specifying
an image (sub-5s allocation). Maps to SandboxClaim CRD.

v1SandboxTemplate.description:
SandboxTemplate defines a reusable Pod template for Sandboxes. It maps to a
K8s SandboxTemplate CRD (extensions.agents.x-k8s.io/v1beta1).

inputSchemaJson.title: JSON Schema for the tool's input parameters

caCert.description: PEM-encoded CA bundle. Optional; when empty, system roots are used.

token.description: Bearer token. Hub stores it AEAD-encrypted; never returned.

ownerName.description:
Human-readable owner name (e.g. Casdoor displayName). Populated at skill
creation from the authenticated principal; empty for skills created before
this field existed (clients fall back to owner_id).

relation.description:
relation is optional. Use it to express "member of group X" as
subject = {type:"group", id:"g_1", relation:"member"}.

v1VisibilitySyncStatus.description:
VisibilitySyncStatus records the SpiceDB projection state for visibility
(design §7.5). Reconcile converges non-SYNCED rows toward SYNCED along the
DB desired direction.

v1WarmPool.description: WarmPool pre-warms N sandbox pods for sub-5s allocation via SandboxClaim.

parameters.description: Filter fields. Empty fields are wildcards.
parameters.name: actorId

get.summary:
QueryAuditRecords returns audit records matching the filter.
Results are sorted by time descending (newest first).

post.description:
Supports PKCE via the optional `code_verifier` field for public clients
(SPAs, mobile apps) that cannot safely store a client secret. When the
authorization request was made with a `code_challenge`, the same
`code_verifier` MUST be supplied here.

NOTE: kernel authn exposes CodeVerifier on AuthCodeExchangeRequest, but
the current Casdoor SDK adapter (kernel/authn/casdoor/token.go) does not
yet forward it to GetOAuthToken. The field is accepted on the wire for
forward compatibility; verify the kernel version before relying on PKCE
enforcement server-side.
post.summary: Exchange exchanges an OAuth authorization code for IdP tokens.

post.description:
Useful for debug tools, third-party integrations, and service-to-service
calls that need to verify a token's validity and identity without going
through the full request middleware chain. Mirrors RFC 7662 token
introspection semantics (active=false when the token is invalid,
expired, or revoked).
post.summary: Introspect validates a token and returns the principal it represents.

get.description:
Browser users should prefer GET /v1/authn/login, which is registered as a
direct HTTP 302 route by the service implementation. See the service
comment above.

parameters.description:
org_id optionally overrides the globally configured Casdoor organization
for this login URL. When unset, the global config organization is used.
Maps to kernel authn.LoginURLRequest.OrgID.
parameters.name: orgId

parameters.description:
app_id optionally overrides the globally configured Casdoor application.
Maps to kernel authn.LoginURLRequest.AppID.
parameters.name: appId

get.summary: LoginURL returns the IdP authorization URL as JSON for SPA/debug usage.

get.description:
Browser users can also call GET /v1/authn/logout for a direct HTTP 302.

Side effect: token revocation. When the request carries an
`Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` header, OR an explicit
`access_token` field is supplied in the request body, the implementation
calls Revoke on that access token before returning the IdP logout URL.
This adds the token to the local session blacklist so subsequent API
calls with the same token are rejected even before the IdP session
expires. The `access_token` field makes this previously-implicit side
effect visible in the API contract.


The logout URL is built by kernel authn.LogoutService (implemented by
the Casdoor adapter in kernel/authn/casdoor). The hub data layer
delegates to Resources.LogoutService.BuildLogoutURL().

parameters.description:
access_token explicitly identifies the token to revoke locally before
returning the IdP logout URL. When unset, the implementation falls back
to the `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>` request header.

This field makes the implicit revocation side effect of LogoutURL
visible in the API contract instead of being hidden in service code
(legacy backend internal/biz/auth.go:LogoutURL).

Revoke is best-effort. If the local session manager is unavailable the
implementation MUST still return the logout URL and log a warning.
parameters.name: accessToken

get.summary: LogoutURL returns the IdP logout URL as JSON for SPA/debug usage.

get.description:
Requires `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`. The returned Principal
includes `expires_at` so frontends can schedule proactive token refreshes
before the token expires, rather than waiting for a 401 to trigger a
reactive refresh.
get.summary: Me returns the current authenticated principal.

post.summary: Refresh refreshes an IdP access token using a refresh token.

post.description:
Use this for multi-device session management (e.g. "sign out everywhere"
flows) where the caller wants to invalidate a token without navigating
the IdP logout page.

IdP-side revocation is best-effort; the local session blacklist is
always updated so the token is rejected at the next API call regardless
of whether the IdP actually revokes it.

NOTE: the current Casdoor SDK adapter (kernel/authn/casdoor/token.go)
returns ErrIdentityBackendFailed("casdoor sdk does not expose token
revocation"). Until the kernel adapter is updated, this RPC will only
update the local blacklist and return a warning. Callers that need
full IdP-side revocation should use LogoutURL with id_token_hint.

parameters.description: RevokeRequest asks the implementation to revoke a single token.
parameters.name: body

post.summary: Revoke explicitly revokes an access or refresh token.
CheckPermission evaluates whether subject has permission on resource.

BatchCheckPermissions evaluates multiple checks in one round-trip.
Useful for list views where each row needs its own permission check.

parameters.description:
relation is optional. Use it to express "member of group X" as
subject = {type:"group", id:"g_1", relation:"member"}.
parameters.name: subject.relation

get.summary:
LookupResources returns resource IDs that the subject can access
with the given permission. Used by "list my skills" views.

LookupSubjects returns subjects that can access the given resource
with the given permission. Used by "who can access this skill?" views.

ReadRelationships lists relationship tuples matching the filter.
Used by share-management UIs (e.g. "who has access to skill X?").

post.summary:
WriteRelationships creates or updates relationship tuples. Idempotent
(TOUCH semantics): writing an existing tuple is a no-op.

DeleteRelationships removes relationship tuples matching the filter.
At least one of resource / subject filter field must be set.

get.summary: ReadSchema returns the current SpiceDB schema text.

put.summary:
WriteSchema replaces the SpiceDB schema. Use with care — schema
changes are global and may break existing tuples.

parameters.description:
org_id overrides the principal's zone. Requires view_zone in that zone
(design §7.6.4). V1 does not provide cross-zone aggregation; there is
no all_orgs parameter.
parameters.name: orgId

parameters.description: Exact SemVer only; ranges, branches and channels are not accepted.
parameters.name: version
